finding_results.notes: Joel's single uninterrupted legal brief broken into three interrupted turns. Tommy now contributes the ambulance-distance argument (line 73) instead of only making the opening assertion. Paul interrupts Joel twice (lines 79, 83) — once cutting off Joel's initial reasoning, once completing Joel's sentence before Joel can land it. The practical stakes remain legible: ambulance delay (Tommy, line 73), deputy/statements (Joel, line 77), same outcome at 7 AM (Joel, line 81). No single character delivers the full case in one block. Narrator description of Joel's voice trimmed to remove admiring qualifier ('each word placed and weighed before delivery'). Contraction levels verified: Tommy high (gonna, he's, it's), Joel variable (contractions mixed with precision), Paul moderate (still, have to, don't).

summary: Revised the 911-delay exchange (lines 69-85) to fragment Joel's polished legal brief across three interrupted turns, with Tommy and Paul exerting active friction on the reasoning. The practical stakes (ambulance distance, deputy timing, identical outcome for Martin) remain legible but now arrive through messy overlap rather than a single briefed diagnosis. All other dialogue in the chapter was audited for contraction compliance and voice fidelity against the style bible; no additional issues found. No continuity changes introduced.
